body_txt: Alice and Bob are having a conversation. Alice wants to make a point, but to tell Bob straightforwardly wouldn't have the impact she wants to make. So she decides to tell him a story. The story at first seems somewhat random and unrelated to the matter at hand. (This might result in annoyance on Bob's part, especially if something important is going on and it seems like Alice is wasting time, or if this is something she does a lot.) However, once the story is finished, it becomes clear that there is a moral behind it. If Bob still seems confused about the story's relevance, Alice may spell it out for him. Or she may just leave him guessing. The most common term for these stories is the parable, a tale used to illustrate some message. The term often refers to religious parables meant to express spiritual concepts. Of course, it's not limited to that. Old people are very prone to do this, often in the form of a Rambling Old Man Monologue . They have a lot of stories to tell. This also makes it a favorite tactic of The Bore . See also Consummate Storyteller . The musical version of this is Morality Ballad . May overlap with Appeal to Familial Wisdom . See also ...And That Little Girl Was Me .
